analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$77,810 on Apr 23 (9 AM ET). Up from $75K low on Apr 21. BTC dominance 58.1% as altcoins shed 2-3%. Fear & Greed 46 (Fear zone). Total market cap $2.68T, 24h volume $107.16B (below 30-day avg — low conviction).
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index: 46 (Fear zone, -4 from neutral — 'no man's land')
BTC dominance 58.1% — capital rotating into BTC as defensive crypto asset
Spot BTC ETF: 5 consecutive days of inflows, $238M net on Apr 21
Strategy (Saylor) bought 34,164 BTC — largest acquisition since late 2024
KelpDAO $292M exploit drove DeFi TVL down $14B — bearish for altcoins, neutral for BTC
Kevin Warsh (Fed nominee) stressed Fed independence vs. Trump rate-cut pressure — hawkish tone
Iran-US Hormuz tensions ongoing — geopolitical uncertainty headwind
Bitcoin Core v31.0 upgrade released — neutral tech event
FOMC Apr 28-29: J.P. Morgan expects hold at 3.50-3.75%
UK CPI Apr 23: 3.3% YoY (already released) — no major US macro Apr 23-24
analysis.macro_backdrop: Fed funds 3.50-3.75% (on hold). US CPI Mar 2026: +3.3% YoY (+10.9% energy). FOMC Apr 28-29 — hold widely expected. No US CPI/PPI releases on Apr 23-24. Goldman Sachs Bitcoin ETF pending SEC review (no specific decision date).
analysis.technical_note: BTC at $77,810. Recovering from Apr 21 low near $75K. BTC only -0.21% while alts -2-3%. Key resistance $80K round number. Key support $75K / $73K. 24h volume below average = limited conviction on current move.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral: Strong institutional demand (ETF inflows 5 days, Strategy mega-buy) vs. Fear & Greed 46, Iran geopolitical risk, and post-DeFi-exploit caution. BTC acting as defensive crypto safe-haven. Mixed signals — no clear trend. Confidence 0.45 = below 0.50 bot threshold, macro filter INACTIVE this cycle.
